name: PDE5 Mechanism And Selectivity Transfer Extract
population_or_context: Class-level and drug-specific pharmacology sources used to decide when non-tadalafil PDE5 evidence can inform tadalafil off-label hypotheses.
sample_size: Not applicable; mechanistic and comparative pharmacology synthesis.
methods: Integrated current label mechanism/selectivity sections with NCBI class overview and comparative pharmacology review abstracts.
population_profile: Human label pharmacology and review-level mechanism evidence; direct for class mechanism plausibility and agent-specific selectivity limits, indirect for clinical outcomes in healthy off-label users.
tadalafil_exposure: Mixed PDE5 inhibitor pharmacology; tadalafil-specific exposure differs because tadalafil supports daily use and has a 17.5-hour mean terminal half-life in healthy subjects.
class_transfer_limits: Shared PDE5 inhibition supports mechanistic plausibility only. Transfer to tadalafil weakens when the outcome depends on half-life, dosing schedule, tissue exposure, PDE6/PDE11 selectivity, active metabolites, onset/food effects, or drug-specific safety labeling.
indication_context: Mechanistic and regulatory pharmacology framework, not clinical efficacy evidence.
study_design: Narrative synthesis of labels, NCBI class overview, and comparative pharmacology reviews.
comparator_context: Agent-to-agent pharmacology comparison; no direct clinical outcome comparator.
safety_findings: Mechanism supports shared vasodilatory and nitrate/GC-stimulator interaction concern, while selectivity differences support preserving agent-specific adverse-effect caveats.
directness_to_target_population: High for determining class plausibility and transfer limits; low for estimating cognitive, mood, cardiovascular-prevention, exercise, or longevity benefit in healthy men.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Mechanistic similarity can overstate clinical transfer; review abstracts and labels do not establish outcome equivalence across drugs, doses, populations, or durations.
